title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — June 22, 2026
headline: Zero score changes across 20 confirmations as the floor cluster hits its most severe concurrent evidence accumulation of the June series — South Sudan's 2018 peace deal collapse toward total war surfaces for the first time, Sudan's El Obeid drone strikes disable hospitals with 500,000 civilians at named imminent risk, and an OpenAI 42-state AG subpoena five days after its IPO filing opens the largest multistate enforcement action against a frontier AI lab in the benchmark record.
summary: June 22 is a zero-applied, confirmation-dominant cycle: 20 assessments across 3 indexes, 0 score changes, 20 confirmations, and 9 floor or near-floor reinforcements. The defining feature is the simultaneous depth of adverse evidence across four independent geographic crises: Sudan, South Sudan, DRC, and Iran each accumulate escalating findings without triggering score moves because all four are already at or within 2.5 points of the harm floor. South Sudan is the highest-magnitude new finding this cycle — President Kiir's abandonment of the 2018 peace deal, First VP Machar's detention on treason charges, SSPDF aerial bombardment of Jonglei health facilities, and a UN commission warning of total-war trajectory are all first-surfaced in this assessment. Sudan carries within-window drone strikes (Jun 22) disabling hospitals and power infrastructure in El Obeid as the UNSC demand from Jun 20 remains unheeded. DRC's Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak has reached 896 cases and 232 deaths with 70+ medics infected and cross-border spread into Uganda now documented; the July natural peak is approximately 5 weeks away with no approved strain-specific vaccine. Iran confirms at 2.5 on 784 YTD executions and 78+ protesters under imminent death sentence — a 37-year political-execution high. In AI labs, OpenAI (confirmed at 27.2, published 27.5) is flagged under pre-adjudication discipline for the 42-state AG subpoena issued Jun 12 — the broadest multistate enforcement coalition against any AI company in the benchmark record — five days after a confidential IPO S-1 filing; INT and ACC are the watch dimensions; EU AI Act full applicability lands Aug 2, 41 days out. xAI/Grok (floor 0.0) carries a third consecutive cycle of legally distinct adverse evidence streams: the Kim v. xAI whistleblower-retaliation suit, the OPC Canada adjudicated deepfake finding, and a UK MP deepfake suit. Humana (35.2 Developing) confirms at the Jun 21 post-downgrade composite; Apple (59.4 Functional) enters its fourth consecutive Towson NLRB I3 watch cycle. The cycle also documents a methodology application: a Bolivia upgrade proposal generated by the assessor (+9.6, 6.3 → 15.9) was rejected at screening under the baseline-provenance and directionality checks — the proposal's premise that 6.3 predated Bolivia's democratic transition was factually incorrect; the 6.3 was produced by the elected Paz government's own Jun 8 impunity law and the military state of emergency, and within-window evidence is uniformly negative. Bolivia confirms at 6.3.

topSignals.title: South Sudan 0.0 Reinforced: 2018 Peace Deal Collapses toward Total War — UN Commission Documents Aerial Bombardment, 471,000 New IDPs
topSignals.description: The highest-magnitude new finding in this cycle: President Kiir has abandoned the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan. First Vice President Riek Machar has been detained and charged with treason. SSPDF forces have resumed active combat against SPLM/A-IO, with aerial bombardment documented in Jonglei and confirmed hits on health facilities. A UN commission warned in March 2026 that the peace agreement is collapsing toward total war; subsequent fighting has produced approximately 471,000 new IDPs, roughly 199,000 refugees, and approximately 100,000 fleeing to Ethiopia. An early-2026 massacre killed approximately 178 people. South Sudan holds at its absolute harm-flag floor of 0.0 — the floor-limitation methodology applies: fresh adverse evidence reinforces the designation without generating further composite delta. The South Sudan finding is methodologically significant because it represents a new conflict-resumption event, not the continuation of a prior trend — the 2018 peace deal had formally held for nearly eight years before this cycle's evidence.

evidence.quote: resources directed to factional warfare, not root causes of suffering
evidence.claim: South Sudan's state capacity has returned to factional-warfare orientation following the 2018 peace deal's collapse, documented by the UN commission and International Crisis Group.
evidence.source: International Crisis Group: Halting South Sudan's Slide into War

evidence.quote: south sudan peace agreement unraveling moving to total war says un commission
evidence.claim: A UN commission documented that the 2018 peace agreement is unraveling toward total war — the highest institutional-tier warning for South Sudan's conflict trajectory.
evidence.source: JURIST: UN commission warns South Sudan peace deal collapsing

topSignals.title: Sudan 0.0 Reinforced: Jun 22 Drone Strikes Disable Hospitals and Power in El Obeid — UNSC Demand Unanswered, ~500,000 Civilians at Named Imminent Risk
topSignals.description: Within this assessment window (Jun 22), RSF drone strikes have hit El Obeid, North Kordofan, disabling hospitals and power infrastructure — the tenth day of strikes in the sequence. The UNSC formal demand to halt (Jun 20) has produced no change in RSF posture. The UN Fact-Finding Mission's prior determination that the El Fasher takeover involved crimes against humanity, war crimes, and acts that may constitute genocide provides the Tier-5 evidentiary foundation; the El Obeid situation is following the identical pattern at the next target city. Sudan holds at the absolute harm-flag floor of 0.0 — national displacement has reached approximately 14 million, the largest displacement crisis in the world. The precision of the risk specification — named city, documented assault-preparation pattern, formal UNSC action, specific civilian population count — makes this the most immediately specified mass-atrocity risk in the current monitoring portfolio. The floor absorbs all adverse evidence; there is no further composite delta available.

evidence.quote: Security Council warns of mass atrocity risk in El Obeid
evidence.claim: The UN Security Council formally demanded the RSF halt its El Obeid assault, citing imminent mass-atrocity risk to approximately 500,000 civilians.
evidence.source: UN News: Security Council warns of mass atrocity risk in El Obeid

evidence.quote: 29 countries raise alarm about atrocities in El Obeid
evidence.claim: Twenty-nine countries issued a joint warning at the Human Rights Council about escalating atrocities in El Obeid, preceding the Security Council demand.
evidence.source: Al Jazeera: 29 countries raise alarm about atrocities in El Obeid

topSignals.title: OpenAI 27.2 Confirmed: 42-State AG Subpoena Five Days After IPO Filing — Multistate Enforcement Encompasses Minors, Sycophancy, Safety, and Data Practices
topSignals.description: A coalition of 42 state attorneys general served OpenAI a subpoena on June 12, five days after a confidential IPO S-1 filing was reported. The subpoena targets advertising practices, user and health data handling, treatment of minors and senior citizens, model sycophancy, and safety policies — spanning five distinct conduct categories simultaneously. Florida separately filed the first state civil suit on June 1 over alleged inadequate age verification and minor self-harm encouragement; a Florida criminal investigation references ChatGPT and the FSU shooting. Pre-adjudication discipline applies: the subpoena is an information-gathering action, not a finding of wrongdoing. OpenAI confirms at 27.2 (published 27.5, delta -0.3) under the pre-adjudication hold. The affected dimensions are INT (1.7) and ACC (1.9) — both reflect the pattern of harm-acknowledgment and correction largely following external pressure rather than proactive institutional conduct. EU AI Act full applicability arrives Aug 2, 41 days from this cycle; no public OpenAI compliance roadmap has been published. The coincidence of IPO filing and the largest multistate enforcement coalition in AI industry history is the structural tension documented this cycle.

evidence.quote: OpenAI hit with sweeping probe from massive coalition of 42 US state attorneys general
evidence.claim: A 42-state AG coalition — the broadest multistate enforcement action against any AI company in the benchmark record — served OpenAI a subpoena five days after its IPO S-1 filing.

evidence.quote: OpenAI faces investigation from state attorneys general
evidence.claim: The investigation targets advertising, data practices, minor and senior protection, model sycophancy, and safety — five concurrent conduct categories under a single enforcement action.

topSignals.title: DRC 2.3 Confirmed: Ebola Reaches 70+ Medic Infections, Crosses into Uganda — M23/Rwanda Forced Recruitment Overlaps Outbreak Geography
topSignals.description: The DRC Bundibugyo strain Ebola outbreak has reached approximately 896 cases and 232 deaths with 70 or more health workers infected — a medic-infection rate that is straining response capacity in the conflict-affected eastern zones. Cross-border spread into Uganda is now documented by WHO. The outbreak is progressing toward the July natural peak window with no approved Bundibugyo-strain vaccine available; existing therapeutics were developed for the Zaire strain and have limited effectiveness. A concurrent crisis: HRW documented on June 10 that Rwandan forces and M23 have systematically conducted forced recruitment — including of children as young as 12 — abusive detention, sexual exploitation, and summary executions in DRC's eastern provinces. These harms are attributed to Rwanda as the external state actor exercising command-and-control over M23, under the victim/perpetrator attribution convention. DRC's own near-floor state-capacity confirms at 2.3 Critical. The geographic overlap of M23-controlled territory with WHO response corridors is the conversion trigger for a DRC-own-conduct obstruction finding if state-adjacent obstruction of the health response occurs.

evidence.claim: The DRC Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak has crossed into Uganda, with 70+ medics infected and approximately 896 cases and 232 deaths as the July natural peak approaches.

evidence.quote: DR Congo, Rwanda, M23 Forcibly Recruit, Detain Thousands
evidence.claim: HRW documented systematic M23/RDF forced recruitment including children as young as 12, abusive detention, and summary executions — attributed to Rwanda as perpetrator under the benchmark's victim/perpetrator convention.

topSignals.title: Iran 2.5 Confirmed: 784 Executions YTD at 37-Year Political High — 78+ Protesters Under Imminent Death Sentence
topSignals.description: Iran confirms at 2.5 Critical on four concurrent high-severity within-window findings: 784 or more executions year-to-date in 2026 (Iran Human Rights), representing the highest rate of political executions in 37 years; the 125th consecutive week of the 'Tuesdays No to Executions' hunger strikes across 56 prisons; Amnesty International's documentation of 78 or more protesters and dissidents under active death sentence including 41 from the January 2026 protests; and the reported transfer of specific named individuals (including Peyman Ganji) to the Ghezel Hesar execution facility at imminent risk. Iran holds at near-floor. The BND and ACC dimensions remain at floor (1.0) — there is no documented state acknowledgment of harm or correction pathway for any of these conduct categories.

topSignals.title: xAI/Grok 0.0 Reinforced: First AI-Safety Whistleblower-Retaliation Suit Against a Frontier Lab, OPC Canada Adjudicated Finding, UK Deepfake Suit — Three Concurrent Legal Actions
topSignals.description: xAI/Grok carries three legally distinct adverse evidence streams this cycle. First, the Kim v. xAI/SpaceX whistleblower-retaliation suit (filed Jun 9, Santa Clara Superior Court) alleges engineer Devin Kim was terminated days before presenting safety findings, including discrimination risks and WMD-information exposure concerns — the first AI-safety whistleblower-retaliation suit against a frontier AI lab in the benchmark record. Second, the Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada's adjudicated finding that xAI launched its image tool without safeguards against non-consensual sexualized deepfakes, with documented violations confirmed as ongoing — a national-regulator adjudication (Tier 5). Third, a UK Member of Parliament deepfake suit remains active. All three are xAI's own conduct. xAI holds at the absolute 0.0 floor. The three-stream concurrent evidence accumulation is the most legally diverse floor cluster in the AI labs index.

evidence.quote: xAI fired an engineer who raised alarms about Grok safety — new lawsuit claims
evidence.claim: Engineer Devin Kim alleges he was terminated for raising Grok safety concerns including discrimination risks and WMD-information exposure — the first AI-safety whistleblower-retaliation suit against a frontier lab.

evidence.quote: Former xAI staffer says he was fired for questioning Grok safety
evidence.claim: Bloomberg Law independently documented the whistleblower's allegations that safety concerns were suppressed prior to his termination.

topSignals.title: Israel 0.0 Reinforced: Six-State Coordinated Settler-Violence Sanctions Jun 9; Amnesty Jun 10 Documents Ethnic Cleansing as State Policy
topSignals.description: Within the assessment window, six countries coordinated sanctions specifically targeting settler-violence networks (Jun 9) — a multilateral enforcement escalation above prior individual-state actions. France banned a sitting Israeli minister under the same coordinated framework. Amnesty International published on June 10 its characterization of the documented displacement pattern as ethnic cleansing conducted as state policy — a Tier-4 finding from a recognized international human-rights body. Both findings are scored against Israel's own conduct. Israel confirms at the absolute 0.0 floor. The six-state coordination on settler-violence specifically — separate from the ongoing Gaza proceedings — represents a widening of the multilateral accountability record to address domestic-territory conduct patterns.

topSignals.title: Bolivia 6.3 Confirmed: Upgrade Proposal Screened Out — Jun 8 Impunity Law and Military State of Emergency Anchor the Published Score
topSignals.description: An assessor-generated upgrade proposal (6.3 → 15.9, +9.6) was rejected at screening under the baseline-provenance and directionality checks. The proposal's premise — that 6.3 predated Bolivia's democratic transition and should be reset to reflect the elected Paz government's conduct — was factually incorrect. The 6.3 was driven down by the Paz government's own documented conduct: the Jun 8 codified-impunity law authorizing military force against protesters, subsequent killing of 10+ protesters and arrest of 365+, and terrorism charges against union leaders. Within-window evidence is uniformly negative: the 90-day military state of emergency declared Jun 20, 17 deaths including 7 from blocked medical access, and a Jun 22 Air Force relief-flight crash killing 6. No positive countervailing conduct exists that would anchor the proposed 15.9 score. Bolivia confirms at 6.3. The screening rejection demonstrates the benchmark's anti-false-positive discipline: the Venezuela transition-government analogy fails because Venezuela's acting government released prisoners, while Bolivia's elected government enacted new impunity law.

evidence.quote: Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz declared a state of emergency, which gives the military the power to remove blockades that have disrupted food and fuel supplies.
evidence.claim: The elected Paz government declared a 90-day military state of emergency on Jun 20, with within-window deaths from blocked medical access — the evidence that anchors the 6.3 published score.

topSignals.title: Apple 59.4 Confirmed: Fourth Consecutive Towson NLRB Watch Cycle — Congressional Attention Reaches 94 Combined Members
topSignals.description: Apple's Towson, Maryland store — the first unionized Apple Store in the United States — closed on June 20. The International Association of Machinists filed an Unfair Labor Practice charge alleging Apple denied unionized Towson workers transfer rights offered to non-union workers at other closing stores. Fifty-four members of the Congressional Labor Caucus asked the NLRB to investigate; forty or more members separately wrote to CEO Tim Cook on June 2. The Maryland Governor publicly criticized the closure. All events remain pre-adjudication: an NLRB charge is an allegation, not a finding. Apple confirms at 59.4 Functional with the I3 (internal-consistency) watch flag entering its fourth consecutive cycle. The congressional attention volume — 94 combined members of Congress engaged on a single labor dispute — is the highest for any Fortune 500 entity in the current June series.

topSignals.title: Humana 35.2 Confirmed: Post-Downgrade Watch Cycle — DOJ FCA and Barrows Proceedings Active, No New Distinct Evidence
topSignals.description: Humana confirms at 35.2 Developing — the post-downgrade composite applied on June 21. The within-window items (DOJ FCA complaint surviving a motion to dismiss, Barrows v. Humana AI denial class action active, CVS/Aetna $117.7M sector settlement signal) are the same matters that drove the June 21 downgrade and are fully incorporated in the published 35.2 score. No new distinct evidence emerged within the June 22 window. The DOJ FCA adjudication and Barrows merits proceedings remain active as forward confirmation triggers. Humana sits 15.2 points below the Functional band floor, within the Developing band.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Bundibugyo Ebola 896 cases / 232 deaths; 70+ medics infected; Uganda cross-border spread confirmed; no approved Bundibugyo vaccine; July natural peak window ~5 weeks away; M23/Rwanda territory overlapping WHO response corridors.
boundaryWatch.note: DRC at 2.3 Critical through cycle 9 of the Ebola outbreak. The 70-medic infection count and Uganda cross-border spread both represent escalation from cycle 8; contact tracing remains below containment thresholds. The geographic overlap of M23/Rwanda-controlled areas with Ebola response corridors in North Kivu is approaching a point where M23 territorial control could impede burial teams or response personnel — state-adjacent obstruction of the WHO-led response constitutes the conversion trigger for a floor-designation proposal from 2.3.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Jun 8 codified-impunity law; 90-day military state of emergency declared Jun 20; 17 deaths including 7 from blocked medical access; Jun 22 Air Force crash killing 6; 365+ arrests and terrorism charges against union leaders.
boundaryWatch.note: Bolivia at 6.3 Critical as the military state of emergency enters its second week. The Jun 22 Air Force crash occurred on a relief-flight mission — the relief-and-harm duality is the defining feature of the Paz government's conduct this cycle. The impunity law (Jun 8) authorizing military force against protesters remains the primary conduct anchor for the published 6.3; military-on-civilian casualties under that law constitute the conversion trigger for a floor-designation proposal.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Bengali Muslim pushbacks continuing; detect-delete-deport digital surveillance of Rohingya and Bengali Muslim communities; eighth consecutive cycle on active watch at 0.6 points above the Critical floor.
boundaryWatch.note: India at 15.6 Critical sits 0.6 points above the Critical floor through an eighth consecutive monitoring cycle on the same documented pattern. The combination of physical deportation, digital identification infrastructure, and detention at scale represents the most extensively documented single-entity boundary watch in the countries index. The conversion trigger is a large-scale documented atrocity or a formal adjudicated finding of statelessness-through-deportation at scale.

emergingRisks.risk: Sudan's El Obeid drone strikes on Jun 22 disabled hospitals and power infrastructure as the RSF assault continues — the UNSC formal demand from Jun 20 has not altered RSF posture, and approximately 500,000 civilians remain at named imminent mass-atrocity risk with the El Fasher pattern repeating at a second city.
emergingRisks.detail: El Obeid follows the El Fasher pattern precisely: massing, drone strikes on civilian infrastructure, UNSC action, then direct assault. The UN Fact-Finding Mission documented El Fasher as potentially genocide; El Obeid now carries all the same documented precursors. Sudan holds at 0.0 with no further composite movement available. The monitoring significance is regional: an El-Obeid-scale atrocity would compress international humanitarian response capacity simultaneously across Sudan, South Sudan, DRC, and Nigeria.

emergingRisks.risk: South Sudan's 2018 peace deal has formally collapsed — a UN commission documents the trajectory toward total war, with SSPDF aerial bombardment of Jonglei health facilities and approximately 471,000 new IDPs in the current conflict phase.
emergingRisks.detail: South Sudan was the quieter floor entity entering this cycle; the renewed-civil-war finding is the single highest-magnitude new emergence in the June 22 assessment set. The 2018 agreement had survived 8 years; its abandonment eliminates the primary internationally-documented conflict-management structure. If combat extends to Equatoria or Central Equatoria, displacement numbers are likely to exceed current estimates rapidly.

emergingRisks.risk: DRC Bundibugyo Ebola is approaching the July natural peak window with 70 or more medics infected, cross-border spread into Uganda confirmed, and no approved strain-specific vaccine — while M23/Rwanda-controlled territory increasingly overlaps the WHO-led response corridors.
emergingRisks.detail: The medic-infection rate (70+) is the new severity marker this cycle — response-capacity erosion from within. Uganda cross-border spread represents a second country now in the outbreak perimeter. Contact tracing remains below containment thresholds. July is the next critical checkpoint; if M23 territorial control impedes burial teams or response corridors, the Rwanda perpetrator-attribution would extend to a secondary obstruction pathway independent of DRC state conduct.

emergingRisks.risk: OpenAI's 42-state AG subpoena, Florida civil suit, and Florida criminal investigation reference are all pre-adjudication — but the simultaneous IPO filing and multistate enforcement action creates a structural accountability test that will resolve over the coming months, with EU AI Act full applicability on Aug 2 adding a third concurrent regulatory timeline.
emergingRisks.detail: The 42-state coalition is the broadest multistate enforcement action against any AI company in the benchmark record. The IPO filing creates commercial incentives that run in tension with the safety-accountability conduct the subpoena is probing. EU AI Act Aug 2 applicability means the evidence accumulation period runs in parallel with a new regulatory enforcement regime. Three concurrent accountability tracks on a single entity is the highest regulatory-risk concentration in the AI labs index.

emergingRisks.risk: EU AI Act full applicability on August 2 is 41 days away — three AI labs entities (xAI/Grok, OpenAI, Figure AI) carry documented safety-governance gaps mapping onto Article 9 risk-management system requirements for high-risk and GPAI systems, with no public compliance roadmap published by any of the three.
emergingRisks.detail: Post-Aug 2 non-compliance by any EU-facing deployment constitutes a Tier-5 regulatory finding under the benchmark methodology. The contrast with DeepMind/Google (56.9, AI Control Roadmap published Jun 18) and Anthropic (59.1, documented institutional safety infrastructure) is the clearest cross-entity differentiation on regulatory readiness in the current AI labs index.

emergingRisks.risk: Apple's Towson NLRB I3 watch flag has now accumulated four consecutive cycles and 94 combined members of Congress engaged — the congressional attention volume creates a political-accountability track that runs parallel to the NLRB adjudication timeline.
emergingRisks.detail: An adverse NLRB adjudication on the IAM unfair-labor-practice charge would establish the first direct-actor I3 finding in Apple's Fortune 500 record. The congressional engagement — 54 members to the NLRB plus 40+ to CEO Cook — is categorically above the pre-adjudication baseline and represents a Tier-3 evidence-tier strengthening without yet constituting a Tier-4 or Tier-5 adjudicatory finding.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Floor Cluster: Nine Entities Reinforce Simultaneously, Most Severe Concurrent Evidence of June Series
sectorAlerts.headline: Nine floor and near-floor country entities accumulate adverse evidence this cycle, with South Sudan's renewed civil war as the highest-magnitude new emergence and Sudan's drone strikes as the most immediately severe ongoing risk
observations: South Sudan (0.0): First-surfaced this cycle — the 2018 peace deal's formal collapse, SSPDF aerial bombardment of Jonglei health facilities, and ~471,000 new IDPs constitute the benchmark's first renewed-civil-war floor-reinforcement for this entity since 2016.

Sudan (0.0): Jun 22 drone strikes disable hospitals and power in El Obeid; the UNSC demand from Jun 20 is unheeded. The El Fasher → El Obeid pattern is the most precisely specified near-term mass-atrocity sequence in the monitoring portfolio.

Israel (0.0): Six-state coordinated settler-violence sanctions and Amnesty's ethnic-cleansing characterization both within window — the coordinated multilateral response is the most developed accountability infrastructure for any floor entity this cycle.

xAI/Grok (0.0): Three legally distinct adverse streams — whistleblower-retaliation, Canadian regulator adjudication, UK MP deepfake suit — represent the most legally diverse concurrent evidence base in the AI labs floor cluster.
DRC (2.3): Medic-infection rate (70+) is the new severity escalator; Uganda cross-border spread adds a second country to the outbreak perimeter. July peak is the critical near-term checkpoint.

Iran (2.5): 125th hunger-strike week; 784+ executions; 78+ under imminent death sentence. The execution rate is at its highest political level in 37 years — a sustained, not episodic, conduct pattern.
Bolivia (6.3): Upgrade proposal screened out on baseline-provenance check — 6.3 accurately reflects the elected Paz government's own conduct, not a stale prior-regime baseline.
Nigeria (18.0): 52.8M acutely food-insecure in the 2026 lean season — the highest food-insecurity count Nigeria has produced in the benchmark monitoring record.

Venezuela (18.0): Partial prisoner releases (659 cumulative) partially offset by 400+ remaining detainees and intact authoritarian structures — the +0.4 sub-threshold delta is the only upward movement in the floor cluster this cycle.

sectorAlerts.sector: AI Labs — Regulatory Convergence: Multistate AG Action, Adjudicated Privacy Finding, and EU AI Act Applicability Arriving Simultaneously
sectorAlerts.headline: Three concurrent regulatory actions across two AI labs (OpenAI, xAI) and an approaching EU AI Act deadline create the highest regulatory-risk concentration in the AI labs index in the current monitoring series
observations: OpenAI (27.2): The 42-state AG subpoena — the broadest multistate AI enforcement action in the benchmark record — coincides with IPO preparation. INT (1.7) and ACC (1.9) are the affected dimensions; both reflect reactive rather than proactive harm-acknowledgment patterns.

xAI/Grok (0.0): The Kim v. xAI whistleblower-retaliation suit is the first of its kind in the AI industry — a direct-actor INT floor anchor that the pre-existing OPC Canada adjudication now supports from a separate evidence category.

EU AI Act full applicability (Aug 2): Three AI labs entities lack public compliance roadmaps for Article 9 risk-management requirements; the contrast with DeepMind (AI Control Roadmap) and Anthropic (documented safety infrastructure) represents the sharpest compliance-readiness gap in the index.

The five-point spread within the AI labs index's accountability dimension (ACC) between the top tier (Anthropic 59.1, DeepMind 56.9) and the floor (xAI 0.0, OpenAI 27.2) is the widest documented inter-entity ACC spread in the current series.

sectorAlerts.sector: Fortune 500 — Healthcare and Technology: Post-Downgrade Watch and Fourth-Cycle Labor Watch
sectorAlerts.headline: Humana confirms at the applied 35.2 Developing score while Apple enters its fourth consecutive NLRB watch cycle — both pre-adjudication tracks remain active with distinct forward triggers
observations: Humana (35.2): Post-downgrade confirmation with no new distinct evidence. The DOJ FCA complaint and Barrows class action are both active. The next substantive move trigger is an adjudicatory outcome — FCA ruling or Barrows merits ruling — neither of which has a fixed date.

Apple (59.4): Congressional engagement volume (94 combined members) is the highest for any Fortune 500 entity on a single labor dispute in the June 2026 series. The pre-adjudication discipline continues to hold the score; an adverse NLRB adjudication would be the first direct-actor I3 finding in Apple's Fortune 500 record.

Five rotation-backfill Fortune 500 entities (Tuesday Morning, GNC Holdings, Oasis Petroleum, Denbury Resources, W&T Offshore) confirm at the 23.4 Developing placeholder composite with low confidence and no 14-day window evidence — consistent with the defunct/restructured-entity pattern seen in prior rotation cycles.

methodologyNotes.name: ANTI-FALSE-POSITIVE SCREENING — Bolivia Upgrade Proposal Rejected on Baseline-Provenance + Directionality Check (June 22)
methodologyNotes.description: An assessor-generated upgrade proposal for Bolivia (6.3 → 15.9, +9.6) was rejected at screening. The proposal analogized Bolivia to the Venezuela special-case baseline-reset, arguing that the published 6.3 predated the Nov-2025 democratic transition. The baseline-provenance check refuted this: the 6.3 was driven down to 6.3 by the elected Paz government's own conduct — specifically the Jun 8 codified-impunity law authorizing military force against protesters, the subsequent killing of 10+ protesters and arrest of 365+, and the 90-day military state of emergency declared Jun 20. The directionality check confirms: all within-window evidence is negative (17 deaths from blocked medical access, Jun 22 relief-flight crash killing 6). No positive countervailing conduct exists to anchor 15.9. The Venezuela analogy fails because Venezuela's transition government released 659 political prisoners; Bolivia's transition government enacted new impunity law. Bolivia confirms at 6.3. This is the benchmark's first exercise of the anti-false-positive discipline on a generated-then-rejected upgrade proposal.

methodologyNotes.name: FLOOR-LIMITATION — NINE ENTITIES CONFIRMED (South Sudan, Sudan, Afghanistan, Israel, xAI/Grok, Iran, DRC, Bolivia, Nigeria)
methodologyNotes.description: Nine entities confirm at floor or near-floor this cycle under the floor-limitation methodology. Adverse evidence reinforces existing floor designations but cannot generate further composite delta when dimensional headroom is exhausted. South Sudan's renewed-civil-war finding is the highest-magnitude new emergence; Sudan's Jun 22 drone strikes are the most immediately acute within-window event. The floor cluster is the largest simultaneous floor-reinforcement set in the June 2026 series by entity count.

methodologyNotes.name: PRE-ADJUDICATION DISCIPLINE — OPENAI 42-STATE AG SUBPOENA, FLORIDA CIVIL SUIT, FLORIDA CRIMINAL INVESTIGATION REFERENCE
methodologyNotes.description: A 42-state AG subpoena, a Florida civil suit, and a Florida criminal investigation reference are all pre-adjudication enforcement actions — none constitutes a finding of wrongdoing. Under pre-adjudication discipline, these are recorded as watch flags (INT and ACC dimensions) and an evidence-tier upgrade to Tier-4 (state AG enforcement action), but do not by themselves move the score below the 5-point threshold. OpenAI confirms at 27.2 (published 27.5, delta -0.3). The evidence tier is now at its highest level for OpenAI in the June 2026 series; resolution of the AG probe or the Florida suit would constitute the adjudicatory trigger for a scored change.

forwardTriggers.trigger: RSF commencement of direct assault on El Obeid residential zones — the El Fasher pattern predicts this as the next phase following drone strikes on infrastructure. Sudan at absolute 0.0 floor; monitoring significance is the most precisely specified near-term mass-atrocity risk in the current portfolio.

Expansion of SSPDF aerial bombardment to Equatoria or Central Equatoria — would trigger mass displacement escalation beyond current ~471,000 IDP count and draw in cross-border refugee flows. South Sudan at absolute 0.0 floor.

July natural Bundibugyo Ebola peak window; 896 cases / 232 deaths as of latest data; 70+ medics infected; Uganda cross-border spread confirmed; no approved Bundibugyo vaccine; M23 territory overlapping WHO response corridors is the secondary conversion trigger for DRC own-conduct floor designation.

forwardTriggers.entity: EU AI Act — AI Labs Index
forwardTriggers.trigger: EU AI Act full applicability for high-risk and GPAI provisions (August 2, 41 days from June 22). OpenAI, xAI/Grok, and Figure AI each have documented safety-governance gaps mapping onto Article 9 risk-management requirements. Post-applicability non-compliance by an EU-facing deployment constitutes a Tier-5 regulatory finding.

42-state AG investigation resolution OR Florida civil suit adjudication (minor self-harm, age verification) OR Florida criminal investigation outcome. Any adjudicatory finding would cross the pre-adjudication threshold and trigger a scored change from 27.2.

DOJ False Claims Act ruling or settlement OR Barrows v. Humana merits ruling on the nH Predict AI denial class action. Either constitutes adjudicatory confirmation of the applied 35.2 Developing score. Proceedings remain open.

August 2026 IPC lean-season update — primary checkpoint for Borno State Phase 5 famine trajectory; 52.8M food-insecure baseline entering the peak lean window. Secondary: ISWAP insurgency pressure on humanitarian-access corridors.

NLRB adjudication of IAM unfair-labor-practice charge over Towson closure (transfer-rights disparity between unionized and non-union stores). Adverse ruling would establish the first direct-actor I3 finding in Apple's Fortune 500 record. Fourth consecutive cycle on I3 watch.

Military-on-civilian casualties under the Jun 8 codified-impunity law — conversion trigger for a floor-designation proposal from 6.3. Army is deployed but no civilian-targeted casualties have been documented this cycle.

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: If South Sudan's SSPDF extends aerial bombardment to Equatoria province, does the same ACT floor anchor that holds for Jonglei constitute sufficient basis for a formal conflict-resumption methodology notation, or does the 2018 peace deal's documented abandonment alone constitute an INT floor anchor independent of the bombardment's geographic scope?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: South Sudan has held at 0.0 through the peace-deal period; its formal collapse raises whether the floor anchor shifts from conduct-based to structural-commitment-based.
